description: All below-slab and in-wall rough plumbing is installed, inspected, and signed off before concrete pour or wall cover-up proceeds.

categories.name: Plans & Layout Verification
items: Approved plumbing plan on-site and plumber working from current stamped set (not a prior revision)
All fixture locations field-verified against architectural plan — toilets, tubs, showers, sinks, laundry, hose bibbs, wet bar, outdoor kitchen
Drain and vent riser locations confirmed against framing — no conflicts with beams, headers, or engineered lumber
Water supply routing confirmed with HVAC and electrical subs to avoid crossing conflicts in same stud bay or joist cavity

categories.name: Below-Slab Rough-In
items: All below-slab drain lines installed to correct pipe size per IPC schedule — minimum 3-inch drain to water closets, 4-inch building drain to septic/sewer
Below-slab drain lines set to correct fall — minimum 1/4-inch per foot slope verified with level, no flat or back-pitched runs
All below-slab pipe bedded in clean sand per Baldwin County standard — minimum 4 inches below and to sides of pipe
Cleanout locations installed per IPC requirements — within 5 feet of building drain exit and at base of each stack
All penetrations through footings sleeved and sealed — no unsleeved pipe cast directly into concrete footer
Below-slab pressure test completed prior to pour — minimum 10 PSI air or hydrostatic, held 15 minutes, no drop
Baldwin County below-slab plumbing inspection passed and inspection card signed before concrete is poured

categories.name: Drain, Waste & Vent Rough-In
items: All drain, waste, and vent pipe material confirmed correct per spec — schedule 40 PVC or cast iron per client/plan designation; no ABS unless explicitly approved
All water closet flanges set to correct rough-in dimension — 12-inch from finished wall face (or 10-inch / 14-inch per fixture selection confirmed in Precon)
Tub and shower drain locations set per manufacturer rough-in sheet for the specific selected fixtures
All vent pipes continuous and connected to main vent stack — no orphaned drain lines without proper venting
Vent stack penetrations through roof decking sleeved, positioned minimum 10 feet from any operable window or HVAC intake per IRC
All horizontal drain lines supported at maximum 4-foot intervals; vertical stacks supported at each floor per IPC
No notches in load-bearing studs deeper than 25% of stud depth; all notched studs protected with 1/16-inch steel nail plate

categories.name: Water Supply Rough-In
items: All supply pipe material confirmed per spec — PEX-A, copper Type L, or CPVC per plan and client selection; no PEX-B substituted without superintendent approval
Manifold location installed per plan — accessible location, labeled per fixture, isolation valve on each leg
Hot and cold supply stubs set at correct height and spacing for each fixture — verify against manufacturer rough-in sheet for selected faucets, especially wall-mount and freestanding tub filler
All supply lines secured every 32 inches horizontally and 48 inches vertically; no unsupported spans that allow pipe movement or hammering
Water hammer arrestors installed at washing machine, dishwasher, and any quick-closing solenoid valve locations per ASSE 1010
All supply line penetrations through framing protected with nail plates — any pipe within 1.25 inches of face of stud or joist
Pressure test on supply system completed — minimum 80 PSI static, held 4 hours, verified by superintendent and documented with photo

categories.name: Specialty & Mechanical Connections
items: Gas line rough-in (if natural gas or propane) stubbed to correct locations — range, tankless water heater, fireplace, outdoor kitchen, generator pad
Tankless or tank water heater location confirmed — verified adequate space for code-required service clearances and condensate drain (if applicable)
Recirculation loop installed if specified in plans — loop return stubbed at water heater and each fixture cluster per design
Exterior hose bibb locations confirmed and freeze-proof bibb model verified as specified — minimum two per plan, additional per client selection
Ice maker, pot filler, and steam unit supply lines stubbed to correct location per appliance rough-in sheets
Floor drain installed in mechanical room and any other location indicated on plan — trap primed or trap guard installed

categories.name: Inspection & Pre-Cover Sign-Off
items: Baldwin County rough plumbing inspection scheduled and passed — inspection card physically on-site and signed
Superintendent walked all rough-in with plumber before inspection call — all open pipe ends capped, no exposed debris inside lines
Photo documentation complete — above-slab rough-in photographed by zone and uploaded to project Drive folder before any wall cavities are closed
Framing superintendent confirmed no structural members were over-notched or drilled out of tolerance to accommodate plumbing routing
Plumber sign-off sheet signed and dated confirming system is ready for cover — on file in project binder
Superintendent final walk completed and any punch items from inspection or site walk resolved and re-verified before drywall phase is released to proceed
